title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement joshua.marie.k@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

what: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: Higher Level Interface Statistical Inference

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: statim mean? statim Latin word “immediately, ”. name carries double meaning: stat: statistics, domain package lives im (statim): immediate, signalling inference expressible direct declaration, somewhat sequence mechanical steps simply means: declare statistical inference want perform, statim immediately delivers .

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: package yet submitted CRAN. time , can install current implementation GitHub:
code: # Stable version (not yet released) install.packages("statim") # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("joshuamarie/statim")

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: statim provides human-readable, easy--write syntax. statim much higher-level package, inherits strongest features R makes code easy write understand, dplyr / tidyr’s use <tidyselect-helpers>. re-imagines grammar statistical inference implementation R. embodies almost paradigm ggplot2, except enforces use actual pipes, +. ’s example quick H-test pipeline: tidyverse-like grammar semantics bottom part code part “pipeline” re-calibrated classical t-test procedure, permutation t-test. 1-liner syntax better since can re-calibrate declaration H-test want perform. Otherwise, (2) want eagerly run actual H-test.

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: package designed around three ideas: Declarative models: describe structure data define_model() model IDs like x_by(), rel(), pairwise() Composable pipeline: build test specification lazily, execute conclude() Extensible implementations: every test test_define() object; bring engine, method, implementation

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference
text: MIT © Joshua Marie

title: statim • Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference

what: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
title: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
text: HTEST_FN() developer-interface function, constructor user-facing test functions like TTEST(). returns function consistent signature routes correct implementation based model ID method variant.

title: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
code: HTEST_FN(cls, defs, .name)

title: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
text: cls string naming test class, e.g. "ttest". defs list test_define objects declaring implementations. .name string used test title output.

title: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
text: function signature function(.model, .data, ..., .extra_defs).

title: Build a hypothesis test function — HTEST_FN
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ MY_TEST = HTEST_FN(     cls = "mytest",     defs = list(my_def_two),     .name = "My Test" ) } # }

title: T-Test — TTEST
text: TTEST() performs t-test one-sample, two-sample, paired, pairwise, formula-based comparisons.

title: T-Test — TTEST
code: TTEST(.model = NULL, .data = NULL, ..., .extra_defs = list())

title: T-Test — TTEST
text: .model model ID x_by(), pairwise(), formula. supplied, test executes immediately. NULL (default), returns test_spec use pipeline via prepare_test(). .data data frame. used standalone path. ... Additional arguments passed implementation: .paired, .mu, .alt, .ci classical path. .extra_defs list additional test_define objects supplied user. extend available implementations engines.

title: T-Test — TTEST
text: htest_spec object (standalone eager), test_spec object (pipeline).

title: T-Test — TTEST
text: x_by() — two-sample paired t-test

title: T-Test — TTEST
text: "boot" — bootstrap confidence interval via via()

title: T-Test — TTEST

what: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
text: conclude() terminal step pipeline. resolves method variant engine, builds execution context, runs implementation, returns htest_spec object.

title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
code: conclude(.x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'test_lazy' conclude(.x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'engine_set' conclude(.x, ...)

title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
text: .x test_lazy engine_set object produced prepare_test() (optionally followed () /via()). ... Currently unused.

title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
text: htest_spec S3 object.

title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude
text: engine method variant resolved order: via() called, method_name engine win. () called (producing engine_set), engine used method "" (classical path). Otherwise engine = "default" method = "" used.

title: Execute a lazy test pipeline — conclude

what: Eager execution of the tests — run_htest
title: Eager execution of the tests — run_htest
text: eagerly executes test

title: Eager execution of the tests — run_htest
code: run_htest(defs, args, cls, model_id, .data, .name)

title: Eager execution of the tests — run_htest
text: allowed run H-test function, e.g. TTEST(extra ~ group, sleep), eagerly. hood, defs contains list implementations, construct dictionary implemented functions build_lookup(), match find_def(). impl. look-saved def, S7 class, S3 S4. Since eagerly executes test, try rely define_model() process model_id defined. processed directly (thus, processed = model_processor(model_id, data = .data) third line code). Internally, context test lookup infer_context R6 class, intends pass arguments used implementation. , use constructed context def@run() execute test want perform. Save raw output new class, new_htest() typically. .name name test, optional actually.

what: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
text: fun_args() declares arguments accepted test_define() run function, along default values. Pass result fun_args property test_define().

title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
code: fun_args(...)

title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
text: ... Named arguments defaults (name = value) one-sided formulas required arguments (~name).

title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
text: fun_args object — named list element carries name, default, required fields.

title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
text: Arguments declared one two ways: name = value — argument default value ~name — required argument default Declared defaults used ic_arg() fallbacks user supply value. Required arguments cause ic_arg() error supplied.

title: Declare arguments for a test implementation — fun_args
code: # all with defaults fun_args(.paired = TRUE, .mu = 0, .alt = "two.sided", .ci = 0.95) #> $.paired #> $.paired$name #> [1] ".paired" #>  #> $.paired$default #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $.paired$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $.mu #> $.mu$name #> [1] ".mu" #>  #> $.mu$default #> [1] 0 #>  #> $.mu$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $.alt #> $.alt$name #> [1] ".alt" #>  #> $.alt$default #> [1] "two.sided" #>  #> $.alt$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $.ci #> $.ci$name #> [1] ".ci" #>  #> $.ci$default #> [1] 0.95 #>  #> $.ci$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fun_args"  # mixed — .ci has a default, .paired is required fun_args(.ci = 0.95, ~.paired) #> $.ci #> $.ci$name #> [1] ".ci" #>  #> $.ci$default #> [1] 0.95 #>  #> $.ci$required #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $.paired #> $.paired$name #> [1] ".paired" #>  #> $.paired$default #> NULL #>  #> $.paired$required #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fun_args"  # used inside test_define() if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ new_def = test_define(     model_type = "x_by",     impl_class = "new_def_in_two",     fun_args = fun_args(         .paired = TRUE,         .mu = 0,         .alt = "two.sided",         .ci = 0.95     ),     vars = list(         x = function(p) p$x_data[[1]],         group = function(p) p$group_data[[1]]     ),     run = function(self) {         paired = ic_arg(self, ".paired")         ci = ic_arg(self, ".ci")     } ) } # }

title: Access data and arguments inside a test implementation — infer-context-accessors

title: Access data and arguments inside a test implementation — infer-context-accessors
code: ic_pull(x, role)  ic_name(x, role)  ic_arg(x, name, default = NULL)  ic_method_arg(x, name, default = NULL)  ic_claim(x, name)

title: Access data and arguments inside a test implementation — infer-context-accessors
text: x infer_context object passed self inside run. role string naming variable role declared vars. E.g. "x", "group". name string naming argument claim. default fallback value argument supplied.

title: Access data and arguments inside a test implementation — infer-context-accessors
text: ic_pull() — vector ic_name() — string ic_arg() — argument value default ic_method_arg() — method argument value default ic_claim() — ClaimDef object NULL

title: Access data and arguments inside a test implementation — infer-context-accessors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ test_new_def = test_define(     model_type = "x_by",     impl_class = "test_new_def_in_two",     vars = list(         x = function(p) p$x_data[[1]],         group = function(p) p$group_data[[1]]     ),     run = function(self) {         grp = as.character(ic_pull(self, "group"))         resp = ic_pull(self, "x")         n = ic_method_arg(self, "n")         ci = ic_arg(self, ".ci", 0.95)     } ) } # }

what: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
title: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
text: infer_context() constructs execution context passed self run function test_define() object. built inside conclude() eager path HTEST_FN() never exposed end user.

title: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
code: infer_context(   processed,   args = list(),   extractors = list(),   fun_args = NULL,   claims = NULL,   method_args = NULL )

title: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
text: processed named list returned model_processor(). Contains subsetted data resolved variable names model ID. args named list test arguments supplied user via ... test function, via update(). extractors named list extractor functions declared test_define() via vars property. function takes processed returns variable role. fun_args fun_args object fun_args() declaring default values required status test argument. NULL implementation declare fun_args. claims named list resolved ClaimDef objects write_claim(). NULL claims declared. method_args named list method-level arguments supplied via via() update(). E.g. n, seed, engine.

title: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
text: infer_context S3 object.

title: Execution context for test implementations — infer_context
text: Extension authors interact context exclusively accessor functions ic_pull(), ic_arg(), ic_method_arg(), ic_name(), ic_claim(). Direct access list fields part public API may change without notice.

what: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
text: inlines() multi-expression analogue c() inline data. c(x1, x2) selects multiple variables columns name data frame calling environment, inlines() accepts raw expressions — vectors, function calls, R expression — evaluates immediately model definition time.

title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
code: inlines(...)

title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
text: ... Named unnamed expressions. named, supplied name becomes variable name processed output. Unnamed elements auto-named role position: xv1, xv2, ... role "x"; grpv1, grpv2, ... role "group"; pv1, pv2, ... inside pairwise(). Names can mixed freely — unnamed elements take auto-name based position regardless whether elements named.

title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
text: named list quosures. Intended use inside model ID functions x_by() rel(); typically called .

title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
text: Use inlines() data live data frame. single inline expression, use () instead. Take note inlines() return evaluated value, naked unevaluated expression.

title: Inline multiple expressions in a model ID — inlines
code: # Named inline expressions — names appear in output x_by(     inlines(x1 = rnorm(30), x2 = rnorm(30)),     I(grp = rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15)) ) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : <inlines> | <inline>   # Unnamed — auto-named as xv1, xv2 under role "x" x_by(     inlines(rnorm(30), rnorm(30)),     I(rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15)) ) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : <inlines> | <inline>   # Mixed — named elements keep their name, unnamed get auto-names x_by(     inlines(x1 = rnorm(30), rnorm(30)),     I(rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15)) ) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : <inlines> | <inline>   # Contrast with c() — selects existing variables by name x1 = rnorm(30) x2 = rnorm(30) grp = rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15) x_by(c(x1, x2), grp) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : x1, x2 | grp

what: Declare a method variant for a test implementation — method_spec

title: Declare a method variant for a test implementation — method_spec
text: name string naming method. E.g. "boot", "permute". Must match .method argument passed via(). method_type string naming method type. E.g. "bootstrap", "replicate", "bayes". defaults named list default arguments method. E.g. list(n = 1000L, seed = NULL).

title: Declare a method variant for a test implementation — method_spec
text: method_spec S7 object.

title: Extract metadata from a model ID — model_id_info
code: # without processed — no vars, no counts model_id_info(x_by(extra, group)) #> $model_type #> [1] "x_by" #>  #> $args #> [1] "extra | group" #>  #> $other_info #> list() #>   # with processed — includes vars and counts dm = define_model(x_by(extra, group), sleep) model_id_info(dm$model_id, dm$processed) #> $model_type #> [1] "x_by" #>  #> $args #> [1] "extra | group" #>  #> $other_info #> $other_info$x_vars #> [1] 1 #>  #> $other_info$by_vars #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $vars #> $vars[[1]] #> $vars[[1]]$name #> [1] "extra" #>  #> $vars[[1]]$preview #> [1] "<dbl [20]>" #>  #>  #> $vars[[2]] #> $vars[[2]]$name #> [1] "group" #>  #> $vars[[2]]$preview #> [1] "<fct [20]>" #>  #>  #>

what: statim: Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference — statim
title: statim: Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference — statim
text: high-level package, attempting re-imagination "grammar statistical inference" framework package, mixing 'ggplot2' / 'tidyverse' paradigm.

title: statim: Higher Level Interface for Statistical Inference — statim
text: Maintainer: Joshua Marie joshua.marie.k@gmail.com Authors: Antoine Soetewey antoine.soetewey@uclouvain.(ORCID)

title: Define a test implementation — test_define
text: test_define() declares single implementation hypothesis test. Multiple test_define objects passed HTEST_FN() via default_defs. main extension point adding new tests engines.

title: Define a test implementation — test_define
code: test_define(   model_type = character(0),   impl_class = character(0),   engine = "default",   method = NULL,   fun_args = NULL,   compatible_params = character(0),   vars = list(),   run = function() NULL,   eval_claim = NULL,   print = NULL )

title: Define a test implementation — test_define
text: model_type string matching primary class model ID implementation handles. E.g. "x_by", "pairwise". impl_class string naming implementation class. E.g. "ttest_two". Used S3 class vector result. engine string naming engine. Defaults "default". Use different string register alternative engine, e.g. "cpp". method method_spec object declaring method variant default arguments. NULL classical implementations. fun_args fun_args object fun_args() declaring default values required status test argument. NULL implementation use ic_arg(). compatible_params character vector ParamDef types implementation can interpret via write_claim(). E.g. c("MU"). vars named list extractor functions. function takes processed returns variable role. E.g.: list(x = function(p) p$x_data[[1]]). run function signature function(self) self infer_context object. Contains full implementation logic. Use ic_pull(), ic_arg(), ic_method_arg() access data args. eval_claim function signature function(self, claim) interprets ClaimDef implementation. NULL write_claim() supported. print function signature function(x, ...) formats result printing. NULL falls back print(x$data).

title: Define a test implementation — test_define
text: test_define S7 object.

title: Define a test implementation — test_define
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ new_htest_fn = test_define(     model_type = "x_by",     impl_class = "mytest_two",     engine = "default",     vars = list(         x = function(p) p$x_data[[1]],         group = function(p) p$group_data[[1]]     ),     run = function(self) {         grp = as.character(ic_pull(self, "group"))         resp = ic_pull(self, "x")         # implementation logic     },     print = function(x, ...) {         print(x$data)         invisible(x)     } ) } # }

what: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
title: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
text: () sets engine without changing method variant. engine inherited via() called afterwards.

title: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
code: through(.x, engine, ...)  # S3 method for class 'test_lazy' through(.x, engine, ...)

title: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
text: .x test_lazy object. engine string naming engine. E.g. "cpp", "rust". ... Additional engine-level arguments.

title: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
text: test_lazy object.

title: Set the computational engine for a test pipeline — through
code: # The "cpp" engine is hypothetical; replace with a registered engine name. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sleep |>     define_model(x_by(extra, group)) |>     prepare_test(TTEST) |>     through("cpp") |>     conclude()  sleep |>     define_model(x_by(extra, group)) |>     prepare_test(TTEST) |>     through("cpp") |>     via("boot", n = 2000) |>     conclude() } # }

what: Recalibrate the test method variant — via
title: Recalibrate the test method variant — via
text: via() switches lazy pipeline alternative method variant (e.g. bootstrap, permutation) merges user-supplied arguments variant's declared defaults.

title: Recalibrate the test method variant — via
code: via(.x, .method, ...)  # S3 method for class 'test_lazy' via(.x, .method, ..., engine = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'engine_set' via(.x, .method, ..., engine = NULL)

title: Recalibrate the test method variant — via
text: .x test_lazy engine_set object. .method string naming method variant. Must match name passed method_spec() one registered test_define() objects. E.g. "boot", "permute". ... Named arguments forwarded method (override defaults). engine string naming engine use. Defaults engine already set (), "default" none set.

title: Recalibrate the test method variant — via
text: modified test_lazy object recalibrate_spec populated.

title: 'Variable compared by groups' model mapping — x_by
text: x response variable. bare name, c() bare names, tidyselect helper (requires data), (expr) inline data. group grouping variable. rules x.

title: 'Variable compared by groups' model mapping — x_by
text: x_by / model_id S3 object.

title: 'Variable compared by groups' model mapping — x_by
code: # bare names (resolved from environment or data) x_by(extra, group) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : extra | group   # inline data x_by(I(rnorm(30)), I(rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15))) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : <inline> | <inline>   # named inline x_by(I(score = rnorm(30)), I(grp = rep(c("a", "b"), each = 15))) #> -- Model Definition ------------------------------------------------------------  #>  #> Model ID : x_by  #> Args : <inline> | <inline>
